title: Funeral Stud / 1, 2, 3, Release 'Em / Lord I Got Some Things I Need to Say
about:
The Funeral Stud, also known as Lord I Got Some Things I Need to Say and 1, 2, 3, Release 'Em (or One, Two, Three, Release 'Em), refers to a clip of female rapper and singer Neace Robinson performing her song "I Wished that Heaven Had a Phone" at a funeral. Internet users on TikTok labeled Robinson a "stud," an LGBTQ+ slang term for a masculine Black lesbian. Notably, she's heard passionately singing in the clip, "Lord, I got some things I need to say," and "1, 2, 1, 2, 3, release 'em!" which signaled for everyone to release balloons.

The original video was shared on TikTok by Robinson on her account @neacedagr8 in early November 2025, although she originally released "I Wished that Heaven Had a Phone" and its music video in 2019. The 2025 clip became an exploitable green-screen video element in video caption memes and its TikTok sound inspired lip dubs.
origin:
On November 2nd, 2025, rapper Neace Robinson shared a video on her TikTok[1] account, @neacedagr8, of herself performing her 2019 song "I Wished that Heaven Had a Phone"[2] at a funeral. She's seen wearing a white snapback, bow tie, green flannel and chain with a pendant reading, "Team Neace."

She's heard harmonizing over the song's intro, singing, "Lord, I got some things I need to say," and, "One, two, one, two, three, release 'em." When she says the latter line, the funeral attendees release balloons. Over three weeks, the video received over 2.9 million likes.
